categories.name: 个性化联邦学习
categories.why: 面向客户端数据分布差异显著的场景，让每个参与方获得更适合自身的数据模型。
categories.advantages: 能显著缓解 non-IID 带来的性能退化。
categories.disadvantages: 统一评价更难，部署与维护成本更高。

categories.name: 知识蒸馏与异构联邦
categories.why: 解决模型结构不一致、通信预算有限或需要更细粒度知识传递的场景。
categories.advantages: 更灵活地支持异构模型和轻量知识交换。
categories.disadvantages: 蒸馏效果依赖知识载体设计，稳定性和解释性较难保证。

papers.title: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
papers.abstract: Modern mobile devices have access to a wealth of data suitable for learning models, which in turn can greatly improve the user experience on the device. For example, language models can improve speech recognition and text entry, and image models can automatically select good photos. However, this rich data is often privacy sensitive, large in quantity, or both, which may preclude logging to the data center and training there using conventional approaches. We advocate an alternative that leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates. We term this decentralized approach Federated Learning.
papers.summary: 本文提出联邦学习（Federated Learning）框架，使移动设备能协作训练共享模型而无需集中原始数据，从而保护隐私。核心算法FederatedAveraging通过迭代模型平均结合本地SGD，在非IID和不平衡数据上实现高效通信，将所需通信轮次减少10-100倍。实验在多种模型和数据集上验证了其鲁棒性。
papers.idea: 每个客户端在本地数据上执行多步SGD更新后，服务器周期性地聚合客户端模型参数进行加权平均，从而在不共享原始数据的情况下训练全局模型，并大幅减少通信次数。
papers.innovation: 首次提出联邦学习概念以应对移动设备数据隐私和通信挑战，并设计了实用的FederatedAveraging算法，实验证明其在非IID数据下具有显著通信效率。

innovation_points.point: 提出联邦学习概念，将模型训练与原始数据访问解耦，强调数据保留在本地以保护隐私。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要中明确指出“leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates”，并命名为Federated Learning。

innovation_points.point: 设计FederatedAveraging算法，通过本地SGD和周期性模型平均，有效降低通信成本。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要“practical method for the federated learning of deep networks based on iterative model averaging”，以及实验“reduction in required communication rounds by 10–100×”。

innovation_points.point: 系统验证了算法在非IID和不平衡数据分布上的鲁棒性。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要“experiments demonstrate the approach is robust to the unbalanced and non-IID data distributions”。

flow_steps: 服务器初始化全局模型参数
每轮随机选择部分客户端参与
服务器广播当前全局模型给选中客户端
各客户端利用本地数据执行多轮SGD更新，得到本地模型
客户端仅将更新后的模型参数（或差分）上传至服务器
服务器按各客户端数据量加权平均所有上传的模型，更新全局模型
重复上述通信轮次直至模型收敛
papers.applications: 移动设备图像分类；个性化语言模型（语音识别、输入法预测）；其它涉及隐私敏感数据的移动端应用
papers.limitations: 本研究主要在受控实验环境下验证了联邦平均算法的有效性，尚未解决实际部署中面临的客户端数据动态变化、可用性模式差异及错误处理等关键问题。

limitation_points.point: 未考虑实际部署中的客户端数据集变化、可用性相关性及错误更新等实际问题。
limitation_points.evidence: “A deployed federated optimization system must also address a myriad of practical issues: client datasets that change as data is added and deleted; client availability that correlates with the local data distribution in complex ways ... and clients that never respond or send corrupted updates. These issues are beyond the scope of the current work.”

limitation_points.point: 实验基于同步更新和固定客户端集的受控环境，与真实联邦学习场景中的异步、动态特性存在差距。
limitation_points.evidence: “We assume a synchronous update scheme that proceeds in rounds of communication. There is a fixed set of K clients, each with a fixed local dataset.” 以及 “we use a controlled environment that is suitable for experiments, but still addresses the key issues of client availability and unbalanced and non-IID data.”

source_candidates.title: Communication-Efficient Learning of Deep Networks from Decentralized Data
source_candidates.abstract: Modern mobile devices have access to a wealth of data suitable for learning models, which in turn can greatly improve the user experience on the device. For example, language models can improve speech recognition and text entry, and image models can automatically select good photos. However, this rich data is often privacy sensitive, large in quantity, or both, which may preclude logging to the data center and training there using conventional approaches. We advocate an alternative that leaves the training data distributed on the mobile devices, and learns a shared model by aggregating locally-computed updates. We term this decentralized approach Federated Learning.
source_candidates.reason: 来自本地 PDF 首页与摘要区域提取。

source_candidates.title: Distributed Learning, Communication Complexity and Privacy
source_candidates.abstract: We consider the problem of PAC-learning from distributed data and analyze fundamental communication complexity questions involved. We provide general upper and lower bounds on the amount of communication needed to learn well, showing that in addition to VC-dimension and covering number, quantities such as the teaching-dimension and mistake-bound of a class play an important role. We also present tight results for a number of common concept classes including conjunctions, parity functions, and decision lists. For linear separators, we show that for non-concentrated distributions, we can use a version of the Perceptron algorithm to learn with much less communication than the number of updates given by the usual margin bound. We also show how boosting can be performed in a generic manner in the distributed setting to achieve communication with only logarithmic dependence on 1/epsilon for any concept class, and demonstrate how recent work on agnostic learning from class-conditional queries can be used to achieve low communication in agnostic settings as well. We additionally present an analysis of privacy, considering both differential privacy and a notion of distributional privacy that is especially appealing in this context.
source_candidates.citation_text: Maria-Florina Balcan, Avrim Blum, Shai Fine, Yishay Mansour (2012). Distributed Learning, Communication Complexity and Privacy. arXiv.
source_candidates.reason: 标题相似度 0.32，包含 arXiv ID，作者数 4

papers.title: Preservation of the Global Knowledge by Not-True Distillation in Federated Learning
papers.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35
papers.abstract: In federated learning, a strong global model is collaboratively learned by aggregating clients’ locally trained models. Although this precludes the need to access clients’ data directly, the global model’s convergence often suffers from data heterogeneity. This study starts from an analogy to continual learning and suggests that forgetting could be the bottleneck of federated learning. We observe that the global model forgets the knowledge from previous rounds, and the local training induces forgetting the knowledge outside of the local distribution. Based on our findings, we hypothesize that tackling down forgetting will relieve the data heterogeneity problem. To this end, we propose a novel and effective algorithm, Federated Not-True Distillation (FedNTD), which preserves the global perspective on locally available data only for the not-true classes. In the experiments, FedNTD shows state-of-the-art performance on various setups without compromising data privacy or incurring additional communication costs.
papers.summary: 该论文研究了联邦学习中的数据异质性问题，将其视为持续学习中的灾难性遗忘现象。通过实验发现，全局模型在非独立同分布数据下会遗忘先前轮次知识，且局部训练导致遗忘本地分布之外的全局知识。为此，提出FedNTD算法，在本地训练时利用全局模型对本地数据的预测，仅对非真实类别进行知识蒸馏，以保留全局视角，防止遗忘。实验表明，FedNTD在多种设置下达到当时最优性能，且不损害数据隐私或增加通信开销。
papers.idea: 在联邦学习的本地训练阶段，对每个样本的非真实类别（非标签类别），使用全局模型在该样本上的预测输出作为软目标进行知识蒸馏，从而在优化本地数据的同时保留全局分布知识，防止灾难性遗忘。
papers.innovation: 该工作创新性地将联邦学习与持续学习中的遗忘现象进行类比，首次系统分析了联邦学习中的遗忘问题，并提出一种简单有效的局部训练蒸馏方法（FedNTD），仅对非真实类别蒸馏全局知识，无需额外通信且不泄露隐私，同时理论上分析了其对权重对齐和分歧的改善。

innovation_points.point: 首次系统研究了联邦学习中的灾难性遗忘现象，揭示了数据异质性导致全局模型对先前轮次知识的遗忘，以及局部训练对局部外分布知识的遗忘。
innovation_points.evidence: Section 2系统分析了遗忘，包括全局模型预测一致性（Figure 2a,b），以及遗忘与数据异质性的关系（Figure 2c）。

innovation_points.point: 提出FedNTD算法，在本地训练时仅对非真实类别使用全局模型预测作为蒸馏目标，保留全局知识，防止遗忘，且不增加额外通信或泄露隐私。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要和Section 3描述FedNTD核心机制：'preserves the global perspective on locally available data only for the not-true classes'，'utilizes the global model’s prediction on locally available data, but only for the not-true classes'。

innovation_points.point: 从权重对齐和权重分歧的角度分析了FedNTD的工作机制，证明其能改善局部模型更新后的权重方向一致性并减少分歧。
innovation_points.evidence: Section 5分析了知识保留如何改善weight alignment和weight divergence，并提供实验验证。

flow_steps: 服务器将当前全局模型参数分发给选中的客户端。
每个客户端收到全局模型，以其作为教师模型，固定参数。
客户端在本地数据集上进行多轮训练：对于每个样本，前向传播获取本地模型和全局模型的logits。
对全局模型logits进行温度缩放，得到软标签概率分布，但仅使用非真实类别（c ≠ y）对应的概率作为蒸馏目标。
计算本地模型softmax概率与hard标签的交叉熵损失，以及本地模型软化概率与全局非真实类别软标签之间的KL散度损失（mask掉真实类别）。
将两个损失加权求和，反向传播更新本地模型参数。
训练完成后，客户端将更新后的本地模型上传至服务器。
服务器接收所有参与客户端的本地模型，根据各客户端数据量加权平均，得到新的全局模型。
返回步骤1进入下一轮通信，直至收敛。
papers.applications: 数据异构环境下的联邦学习场景，如图像分类、自然语言处理、语音识别等分布式隐私保护模型训练，特别适用于涉及非独立同分布局部数据的边缘设备协同学习。
papers.limitations: The experimental validation is limited to small-scale image classification datasets (CIFAR-10/100), leaving open questions about performance on larger, more complex datasets or other tasks. The analysis of forgetting relies on a specific model architecture and dataset, and its generality is not fully established. Although communication costs are not increased, the potential extra local computational overhead from knowledge distillation is not discussed.

limitation_points.point: Experimental evaluation is confined to CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, which are small-scale image classification benchmarks.
limitation_points.evidence: The experiments described use only CIFAR-10 and mention CIFAR-100, with no results on larger datasets such as ImageNet or tasks beyond image classification.

limitation_points.point: The impact of FedNTD on local computational cost is not addressed.
limitation_points.evidence: The abstract highlights no additional communication costs but omits any discussion of the computational overhead from computing global model predictions during local training.

limitation_points.point: Forgetting analysis is based on a specific shallow CNN and a single dataset, which may not generalize to deeper models or more complex scenarios.
limitation_points.evidence: The forgetting study uses a 4-layer CNN on CIFAR-10, and it is unclear whether the observations hold for modern architectures or other data domains.

source_candidates.title: Preservation of the Global Knowledge by Not-True Distillation in Federated Learning
source_candidates.abstract: In federated learning, a strong global model is collaboratively learned by aggregating clients’ locally trained models. Although this precludes the need to access clients’ data directly, the global model’s convergence often suffers from data heterogeneity. This study starts from an analogy to continual learning and suggests that forgetting could be the bottleneck of federated learning. We observe that the global model forgets the knowledge from previous rounds, and the local training induces forgetting the knowledge outside of the local distribution. Based on our findings, we hypothesize that tackling down forgetting will relieve the data heterogeneity problem. To this end, we propose a novel and effective algorithm, Federated Not-True Distillation (FedNTD), which preserves the global perspective on locally available data only for the not-true classes. In the experiments, FedNTD shows state-of-the-art performance on various setups without compromising data privacy or incurring additional communication costs<sup>1</sup>.

source_candidates.title: Preservation of the Global Knowledge by Not-True Distillation in Federated Learning
source_candidates.abstract: In federated learning, a strong global model is collaboratively learned by aggregating clients' locally trained models. Although this precludes the need to access clients' data directly, the global model's convergence often suffers from data heterogeneity. This study starts from an analogy to continual learning and suggests that forgetting could be the bottleneck of federated learning. We observe that the global model forgets the knowledge from previous rounds, and the local training induces forgetting the knowledge outside of the local distribution. Based on our findings, we hypothesize that tackling down forgetting will relieve the data heterogeneity problem. To this end, we propose a novel and effective algorithm, Federated Not-True Distillation (FedNTD), which preserves the global perspective on locally available data only for the not-true classes. In the experiments, FedNTD shows state-of-the-art performance on various setups without compromising data privacy or incurring additional communication costs.

papers.title: FEDGKD: Toward Heterogeneous Federated Learning via Global Knowledge Distillation
papers.abstract: Federated learning, as one enabling technology of edge intelligence, has gained substantial attention due to its efficacy in training deep learning models without data privacy and network bandwidth concerns. However, due to the heterogeneity of the edge computing system and data, many methods suffer from the “client-drift” issue that could considerably impede the convergence of global model training: local models on clients can drift apart, and the aggregated model can be different from the global optimum. To tackle this issue, one intuitive idea is to guide the local model training by global teachers, i.e., past global models, where each client learns the global knowledge from past global models via adaptive knowledge distillation techniques. Inspired by these insights, we propose a novel approach for heterogeneous federated learning, FedGKD, which fuses the knowledge from historical global models and guides local training to alleviate the “client-drift” issue. In this paper, we evaluate FedGKD through extensive experiments across various CV and NLP datasets (i.e., CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST5) under different heterogeneous settings. The proposed method is guaranteed to converge under common assumptions and outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in the non-IID federated setting.
papers.summary: 针对异构联邦学习中的客户端漂移问题，提出FEDGKD框架。该方法利用历史全局模型的集成知识，通过知识蒸馏引导本地训练，在不引入额外隐私风险、不修改模型结构的前提下，缓解非独立同分布数据导致的模型偏移。在CV和NLP任务上验证了有效性，优于现有基线。
papers.idea: 通过集成历史全局模型参数或预测作为教师，引导本地模型在特征空间上蒸馏全局知识，以正则化本地更新并缓解客户端漂移。
papers.innovation: 提出一种无需代理数据、不修改模型结构、无额外隐私泄露的全局知识蒸馏联邦学习机制，利用历史全局模型的集成提供教师信号，适用于异构模型场景。

innovation_points.point: 利用历史全局模型集成作为教师进行知识蒸馏，引导本地训练。
innovation_points.evidence: 摘要“fuses the knowledge from historical global models and guides local training”；正文“FEDGKD ensembles the historical global models in the server buffer by averaging the model parameters... serves as a guide (teacher) for local training on knowledge distillation.”

innovation_points.point: 无需代理数据、不修改模型结构、无额外隐私信息共享。
innovation_points.evidence: Table I对比中三项均为✗，贡献中强调“without introducing any privacy risks or model structure modification requirements”。

innovation_points.point: 兼容异构模型框架，基于特征空间正则化而非参数空间约束。
innovation_points.evidence: 引言“FEDGKD is compatible with the existing heterogeneous model framework”，方法部分解释为何参数空间方法失效。

innovation_points.point: 提出两种变体：FEDGKD（服务器端参数平均集成）和FEDGKD-VOTE（客户端集成预测）。
innovation_points.evidence: 正文“We also develop FEDGKD-VOTE, by broadcasting the historical global models... to ensemble their predictions on the client side.”

flow_steps: 服务器端维护历史全局模型缓冲区（滑动窗口）。
每轮通信：服务器选取近期全局模型，通过参数平均（FEDGKD）或直接分发（FEDGKD-VOTE）生成教师模型并广播给参与客户端。
客户端接收教师模型，结合本地数据计算知识蒸馏损失（如KL散度）和交叉熵损失联合训练。
客户端上传本地更新后的模型至服务器。
服务器聚合客户端模型得到新全局模型，更新历史模型缓冲区。
papers.applications: 图像分类（CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet）；文本分类（AG News, SST5）；边缘智能隐私敏感场景（自动驾驶、无人机感知、异常检测、医疗等）
papers.limitations: The paper does not explicitly state limitations of the proposed FedGKD method. Potential limitations include reliance on convergence assumptions, memory overhead for historical model buffer, and evaluation limited to small-scale datasets.

limitation_points.point: The convergence guarantee relies on common assumptions (e.g., smoothness, bounded gradients) that may not hold in highly heterogeneous practical settings.
limitation_points.evidence: The proposed method is guaranteed to converge under common assumptions (abstract).

limitation_points.point: The server needs to maintain a buffer of historical global models for ensemble, which introduces memory overhead, especially for large models.
limitation_points.evidence: At each communication round, FEDGKD ensembles the historical global models in the server buffer by averaging the model parameters (Section I).

limitation_points.point: Evaluation is performed only on small‐scale CV and NLP datasets (CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST5); scalability to larger or more complex tasks is not explored.
limitation_points.evidence: We evaluate FedGKD through extensive experiments across various CV and NLP datasets (i.e., CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST5) (abstract).

source_candidates.title: IEEE TRANSACTIONS ON COMPUTERS, VOL. 73, NO. 1, JANUARY 2024
source_candidates.abstract: Federated learning, as one enabling technology of edge intelligence, has gained substantial attention due to its efﬁ- cacy in training deep learning models without data privacy and network bandwidth concerns. However, due to the heterogeneity of the edge computing system and data, many methods suffer from the “client-drift” issue that could considerably impede the convergence of global model training: local models on clients can drift apart, and the aggregated model can be different from the global optimum. To tackle this issue, one intuitive idea is to guide the local model training by global teachers, i.e., past global models, where each client learns the global knowledge from past global models via adaptive knowledge distillation techniques. Inspired by these insights, we propose a novel approach for heterogeneous federated learning, FEDGKD, which fuses the knowledge from historical global models and guides local training to alleviate the “client-drift” issue. In this paper, we evaluate FEDGKD through extensive experiments across various CV and NLP datasets (i.e., CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST5) under different heterogeneous settings. The proposed method is guaranteed to converge under common assumptions and outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in the non-IID federated setting. Index Terms—Heterogeneous federated learning, non-IID, knowledge distillation, edge intelligence.
source_candidates.reason: 来自本地 PDF 首页与摘要区域提取。

source_candidates.title: : Toward Heterogeneous Federated Learning via Global Knowledge Distillation
source_candidates.abstract: Federated learning, as one enabling technology of edge intelligence, has gained substantial attention due to its efficacy in training deep learning models without data privacy and network bandwidth concerns. However, due to the heterogeneity of the edge computing system and data, many methods suffer from the “client-drift” issue that could considerably impede the convergence of global model training: local models on clients can drift apart, and the aggregated model can be different from the global optimum. To tackle this issue, one intuitive idea is to guide the local model training by global teachers, i.e., past global models, where each client learns the global knowledge from past global models via adaptive knowledge distillation techniques. Inspired by these insights, we propose a novel approach for heterogeneous federated learning, FedGKD, which fuses the knowledge from historical global models and guides local training to alleviate the “client-drift” issue. In this paper, we evaluate FedGKD through extensive experiments across various CV and NLP datasets (i.e., CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet, AG News, SST5) under different heterogeneous settings. The proposed method is guaranteed to converge under common assumptions and outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in the non-IID federated setting.
